experiences.intentions: Initial survey: box at (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), making it the safest first objective. It is against the left wall but the wall contains a target at (1,1), so pushing it up is valid and solves one target immediately. Player starts at (1,2), already in perfect position to move left of no box? To push the box at (2,1) upward, the player must stand at (3,1), below the box. The path to (3,1) from (1,2) is open via down to (2,2), down to (3,2) blocked by wall, so instead go right/down/left around the central wall area later. For the immediate single move, best is to begin repositioning toward the lower area. Moving down to (2,2) is safe and starts route to get beneath box (2,1) for a push up later, while avoiding premature contact with box (2,1) from the right, which would push it left into wall corner at (2,0)/(1,1?) impossible. So first step is down.
